title: Parent Portal - Ministry of Education
content:
Understand your child's learning
Understand what your child is learning at school or kura, how they are progressing against the curriculum, and what you can do to support them at home.

### Welcome to the Parent Portal
Understand my child's learning
(Text on screen: Understand your child’s learning. Parent and whānau guide to the National Curriculum. Logos: Te Tāhuhu o te Mātauranga Ministry of Education, Te Poutāhū Curriculum Centre, Te Kāwanatanga o Aotearoa New Zealand Government.)
(Scenes of children in classroom at tables smiling and writing)
Exciting changes are coming to the New Zealand Curriculum and Te Marautanga o Aotearoa!
(Teachers at table with children looking at talking through a workbook and reading. Teacher holding up phonics card with a letter and picture.)
Our curriculum is evidence-based knowledge-rich, and grounded in the science of learning.
(Parent with two children at home colouring and reading together on the couch and floor.)
(Children reading on beanbag, and close up of child writing individual letters.)
As parents and whānau, we want you tobe informed about the new learning that your tamariki are experiencing at school or kura.
(Ministry of Education website shown on a laptop.)
(Closeup of individual scrolling through Te Marautanga o Aotearoa and the New Zealand Curriculum website.)
Our new webspace is your gateway tounderstanding these curriculum changes and what your child is learning at their school or kura.
With the launch of the 'parent-friendly' versions of both curricula, you'll know exactly what your child is learning, why it's important, and when they'll be learning it.
(Website landing page showing the different curriculum to choose.)
(Website page showing the different year levels to choose i.e. Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4 New Zealand Curriculum.)
To navigate the website, choose which curriculum you want to learn more about and then go to your tamariki year level.
(Website popout box.)
(Text on screen: Te Marautanga o Aotearoa year-by-year learning guide. What your mokopuna are learning at kura each year, and how you and your whānau can support them to…)
(Text on screen: Tau 3 – Te Marautanga o Aotearoa.)
For example, if your child is learning te reo Māori and is in Year 3 you would click on the Te Marautanga o Aotearoa year-by-year learning guide and then enter at Year 3.
(Website screenshots showing different kura resources.)
(Text on screen (blue screen): Ko te whānau te pouako tuatahi ā, mā Whata Ako hei tautoko. Whānau are the first teachers, and Whata Ako is here to support.)
Here, you will see the kinds of things your child will be learning throughout the year.
(Laptop showing scrolling through pages of the New Zealand Curriculum.)
Likewise, for children who are learning through the New Zealand Curriculum you will navigate the same way.
(Website popout box.)
(Text on screen: New Zealand Curriculum year-by-year learning guide. What your child is learning at school each year, and how you and your family can support them to achieve.)
(Text on screen: Year 4 New Zealand Curriculum.)
If your child is in Year 4 click on the New Zealand Curriculum and enter at Year 4.
(Mother and father with children on couch reading te reo Māori books at home together.)
But that's not all. There are a variety of practice activities and resources tailored to each year level to support your child's learning at home.
(Laptop screen showing example of an online English book and an online te reo Māori book.)
For example online books in both English and Te Reo that you can read with your child.
(Website popout box.)
(Text on screen: Practising sounds and letters – Tau 1.)
(Screenshot examples of phonics cards for both English and te reo Māori.)
There are a variety of visual and audio resources that support modelling correct sounds for phonics and te reo.
(Website screenshot of an informative video.)
(Closeup of child practising printing the letter g.)
(Text on screen: Learning handwriting, letters to sounds.)
(Teacher holding up phonics card with a letter and picture.)
(Text on screen (blue screen): Whata Ako. Kura: Hihira Weteoro. Phonics for te reo Māori learners.)
You can explore our informative videos that explain our new phonics and Hihira Weteoro checks.
(Teacher in class with group of new entrant children learning to write, sitting at large tables.)
(Father and son on couch reading together.)
These checks provide valuable information for your child to access any additional support they might need.
(Five-year-old girl hanging up her school bag on a hook.)
(Text on screen: Pre-entry school visits.)
(Text on screen (blue screen): Your child’s learning. Preparing for parent-teacher interviews.)
(Teacher smiling with parent at school.)
(Text on screen: Positive relationship with your child’s teacher.)
(Teacher pointing at child’s school workbook.)
Plus, get ready for the first day of school with our helpful guides and learn how to prepare for parent-teacher interviews.
(Website screenshots of English sounds and letters cards sets and te reo reading books.)
Stay connected and support your child's educational journey with our comprehensive resources and advice.
(Laptop with scrolling through of Ministry of Education website.)
As the New Zealand Curriculum and Te Marautanga o Aotearoa continue to get updated, so will the information on this website.
This new webspace is packed full of exciting and valuable resources designed just for you.
(Text on screen: Enjoy, be curious, and experience success.)
(Children at desk in classroom.)
Spend some time exploring and discover new learning for you and your child.
(Text on screen: Discover new learning for you and your child. Visit the website: www.education.govt.nz/your-childs-learning . Logos: Te Tāhuhu o te Mātauranga Ministry of Education, Te Poutāhū Curriculum Centre, Te Kāwanatanga o Aotearoa New Zealand Government.)
- A new qualification for Years 11 to 13 Learn about the new qualification system to replace NCEA. Learn more

### A new qualification for Years 11 to 13
Learn about the new qualification system to replace NCEA.
- About the New Zealand Curriculum and your child Find out about the New Zealand Curriculum and how it supports your child’s learning and achievement at school. Learn more

### About the New Zealand Curriculum and your child
Find out about the New Zealand Curriculum and how it supports your child’s learning and achievement at school.
- Changes to the curriculum and why they matter We are making the most significant changes to our national curriculum since 2007. Learn more

### Changes to the curriculum and why they matter
We are making the most significant changes to our national curriculum since 2007.
- Education Review Office guide to schools The Education Review Office (ERO) guide has tips for talking to your child’s school about what matters most. Learn more

### Education Review Office guide to schools
The Education Review Office (ERO) guide has tips for talking to your child’s school about what matters most.
- Guide for the New Zealand Curriculum – Years 0 to 8 The curriculum guide sets out what your child is learning at school each year and how you and your family can support them to achieve. Learn more

### Guide for the New Zealand Curriculum – Years 0 to 8
The curriculum guide sets out what your child is learning at school each year and how you and your family can support them to achieve.
- Guide for the New Zealand Curriculum – Years 9 to 10 The curriculum guide sets out what your child is learning at secondary school each year and how you and your family can support them to achieve. Learn more

### Guide for the New Zealand Curriculum – Years 9 to 10
The curriculum guide sets out what your child is learning at secondary school each year and how you and your family can support them to achieve.
- Guide to SMART – A new assessment tool for your child Learn about the new online assessment tool schools will start using in 2026. Learn more

### Guide to SMART – A new assessment tool for your child
Learn about the new online assessment tool schools will start using in 2026.
- Guide to your child's reporting and progress Find out how the new New Zealand Curriculum reports work and what they show about your child’s progress. Learn more

### Guide to your child's reporting and progress
Find out how the new New Zealand Curriculum reports work and what they show about your child’s progress.
- Learning Languages in Years 1 to 10 In Years 1 to 6, your child may have the opportunity to learn a new language. Schools are encouraged to offer language learning in Years 7 and 8. Learn more

### Learning Languages in Years 1 to 10
In Years 1 to 6, your child may have the opportunity to learn a new language. Schools are encouraged to offer language learning in Years 7 and 8.
- Maths practice The Maths practice website is for children in Years 3 to 8. It offers quick access to hundreds of activities based on the New Zealand Curriculum. Learn more
The Maths practice website is for children in Years 3 to 8. It offers quick access to hundreds of activities based on the New Zealand Curriculum.
- Phonics checks Phonics checks are available for all schools to carry out with all children in their first year of school. This helps teachers understand how well they are progressing with learning to read. Learn more
Phonics checks are available for all schools to carry out with all children in their first year of school. This helps teachers understand how well they are progressing with learning to read.
- Preparing for parent-teacher interviews – New Zealand Curriculum Information to help you make the most of your time at parent-teacher interviews to discuss your child's learning and wellbeing. Learn more

### Preparing for parent-teacher interviews – New Zealand Curriculum
Information to help you make the most of your time at parent-teacher interviews to discuss your child's learning and wellbeing.
- Relationships and sexuality education Relationships and sexuality education (RSE) is taught within the Health and Physical Education (HPE) learning area of the New Zealand Curriculum. Learn more

### Relationships and sexuality education
Relationships and sexuality education (RSE) is taught within the Health and Physical Education (HPE) learning area of the New Zealand Curriculum.
- Staying safe online Digital safety means using technology in smart and careful ways. So, you and your child can stay safe and be kind online. Learn more
Digital safety means using technology in smart and careful ways. So, you and your child can stay safe and be kind online.
- The hour a day initiative for schools Your child will spend an average of 1 hour each day on reading, 1 hour on writing, and 1 hour on maths if they’re in Years 0 to 8 at a state or state-integrated school. Learn more

### The hour a day initiative for schools
Your child will spend an average of 1 hour each day on reading, 1 hour on writing, and 1 hour on maths if they’re in Years 0 to 8 at a state or state-integrated school.
- The NCEA literacy and numeracy co-requisites Resources and information to support your child achieve their co-requisite assessment. Learn more

### The NCEA literacy and numeracy co-requisites
Resources and information to support your child achieve their co-requisite assessment.
